extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out -> not sent) holds on all reachable executions. The invariant "opted_out implies not sent" is inductive. It holds initially (opted_out=false). Each transition preserves it: t1 sets opted_out:=true but simultaneously sets sent:=false; t2 sets sent:=true but its guard requires not opted_out, so opted_out remains false; t3 (opted_out true) sets sent:=false; t4 sets sent:=false unconditionally. The only transition that can set sent:=true is t2, which is disabled whenever opted_out is true. Once opted_out becomes true (only via t1, which forces sent=false), it never reverts to false, and from then on sent can only be set to false (by t3, t4, or t1) and never to true. Therefore the state opted_out=true AND sent=true is unreachable, and the property holds for the entire input space (including executions up to 6 events).
